title: NoSQL Injection
description: Inserting user-controlled objects directly into MongoDB, Redis, or other NoSQL queries can manipulate query operators and bypass access controls (OWASP A03:2021 / CWE-943).

finding.message: Potential NoSQL injection via '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Validate and sanitize all query parameters; reject keys starting with '$' from user input.
Use typed schema validation (e.g., Mongoose schemas) to enforce expected document structure.
Never spread or merge user-controlled objects directly into database query filters.
OWASP Testing Guide: Testing for NoSQL Injection.
